q: The volume (in m^3) of a cube, each edge of which is 45 m, is:
Find the sum of the squares of x and y, given that the number 83y547x is divisible by 72.

A hollow cylindrical pipe has an outer radius of 7 cm and an inner radius of 5 cm. If the pipe is 42 cm long and made of metal with a density of 5 g/cm^3, what is the mass of the pipe? (Use π = 22/7 and Density = Mass/Volume)
What is the HCF of 32, 28 and 24?
A trader sells an item for Rs 1805, incurring a loss of 5%. At what price should he sell the same item to make a profit of 5%?

A mother's present age is 4 times her daughter's age. After 5 years, the mother's age will be 19/7 times the daughter's age. Find the ratio of the mother's age 1 year ago to the daughter's age 1 year ago.
If 2x + 3y = 8 and 4x^2 + 9y^2 = 32, then find the value of 8x^3 + 27y^3.

Rs 2,037 is divided among Vikas, Tina and Sumit such that if Rs 23, Rs 42 and Rs 52 are deducted from their respective shares, the remaining amounts are in the ratio 14 : 19 : 15. Find Tina's actual share.
Find the simple interest on Rs 2,000 at a rate of 7% per annum for the period from 6 February 2023 to 20 April 2023.
If 10% of 20% of 3/4 of a number is 882, then find the number.

A and B can complete a certain piece of work in 12 days. B and C can complete the same work in 15 days. C and A can complete the same work in 20 days. Find the ratio of the number of days in which A and B working separately can each complete the work.
24, 3 and 16 are the fourth proportional, A and 20 are in continued proportion. What is the value of A?
A, B and C can each do a piece of work in 12, 24 and 8 days respectively. If they all work together, in how much time (in days) will the work be completed?
A trader sells goods at a discount of 65% and even then makes a profit of 82%. Find the ratio of the cost price to the marked price.
A car runs at 70 km/hr for 1 hour and at a speed of 'p' km/hr for 1 1/2 hours. If the average speed is 52 km/hr, find the value of p.
If sin θ = 3/5, then find the value of cos θ. (θ is in the first quadrant.)
A girl goes at a speed of 9 km/hr and returns at a speed of 45 km/hr. Find her average speed for the whole journey.

The price of fuel increased by 50%, 30% and 50% respectively over three consecutive months, but in the fourth month the price of fuel decreased by 65%. What is the percentage change in the price of fuel at the end of the fourth month compared to the original price? (Round to two decimal places.)
The average age of 39 students in a class is 27 years. If the teacher's age is also included, the average age of the whole group becomes 28 years. What is the teacher's age (in years)?

The total price of 5 white pens, 2 red pens and 3 blue pens is Rs 78. The total price of 2 white pens, 3 red pens and 4 blue pens is Rs 72. Find the total price of 1 white pen, 1 red pen and 1 blue pen.
If the marked price of an item is Rs 2,400 and a discount of 16% is given, then what is the selling price of the item?
The average of 13 numbers is 92. If 7 of the numbers are 30, 32, 46, 81, 110, 73 and 20, then what will be the average of the remaining numbers?
In a certain code language, 'some good questions' is written as 'jm ix cd' and 'many good problems' is written as 'cd rc gh'. In that language, how is 'good' written?

Answer the following question based on the given arrangement of numbers. Only count those numbers that are placed exactly between two odd numbers. (Series) 4 1 9 7 8 4 8 5 9 4 6 5 2 3 7 4 2 5 7 6 3 2 4 5 1 3 (DIGIT). How many such even numbers are there, each of which has an odd number placed exactly before it and an odd number placed exactly after it?

Two statements are given followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. Assuming the statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follows from the given statements. Statements: Some pencils are erasers. No eraser is a sharpener. Conclusions: (I) Some pencils are sharpeners. (II) Some pencils are not sharpeners.
If '+' and '-' are interchanged with each other, and '×' and '÷' are interchanged with each other, then what will be the value of the following expression? 115 × 5 - 14 ÷ 6 + 15 = ?
Select the option that has the same relationship between the two pairs of words below, in which both pairs are related in the same pattern. HTU : LQY :: PNC : TKG
If 1 is added to each odd digit and 1 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 3215674, what will be the difference between the highest and lowest digits in the new number thus formed?

Sunil starts from point A and walks 64 km towards the west. Then he turns and walks 60 km towards the north. Then he turns and walks 67 km towards the east. Then he turns and walks 78 km towards the south. Then he turns and walks 3 km towards the east. Then at point B he stops. In which direction and at what distance is point B from point A? (Consider only the straight-line distance.)
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 125 142 157 170 181 ?
Which option should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series, following the same pattern? BOM DQL FSK HUJ ?

Select the option that contains the set of numbers that is similar to the related set of numbers given below. (Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers without breaking them down into their constituent digits. e.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) (106, 368, 237) (114, 336, 225)
If '+' means '×', '-' means '+', '×' means '÷' and '÷' means '-', then what will be the value of the following expression? 11 P 3 S (30 Q 5) P 2 R 15 S 6 P 2 = ?

Refer to the following number series and answer the question that follows (all numbers are single-digit numbers only). (Left) 8 5 3 6 2 4 7 9 1 3 5 7 8 4 2 6 1 9 3 7 5 4 (Right). How many numbers are perfect squares in the series (each number counted only once, regardless of repetitions)?
In a certain code language, KFHL is related to RMOS in a certain way. In the same way, MHJN is related to TOQU. Following the same pattern, to which of the following options is QJLP related?
In a row of 54 people facing north, Mohit sits 22nd from the right end. If Charu sits 15th from the left end of the row, how many people sit between Mohit and Charu?

Six persons A, B, C, D, E and F are sitting in a row facing the north direction. D is sitting immediately to the left of B. E sits at one of the extreme ends. There are two persons between B and C. A is sitting immediately to the right of C. Who is sitting at the immediate right of E and immediately to the left of A?
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series of numbers, following the same pattern? 23, 24, 26, 32, 56, 176, ?

On the basis of the given arrangement of letters, three of the following four pairs of letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and form a group. Which one does NOT belong to that group? (i.e. which pair is not related in the same way as the others?)
Select the option that has the same relationship between the two pairs of words below, following the same pattern. AXB : FCG :: IFJ : NKO

Six persons G, H, I, Q, R and S are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. There are three persons between G and S. R is sitting second to the right of G. Q is sitting immediately to the right of G. H is sitting immediately to the right of S. Between which two persons is R sitting?

In a certain code language, A + B means 'A is the wife of B'; A - B means 'A is the brother of B'; A × B means 'A is the father of B'; and A ÷ B means 'A is the sister of B'. If 'T + O & R + N - M', then how is T related to M?
o: T is the wife of M's brother.

q: Answer the following question based on the given arrangement of numbers. (Count only those numbers that are placed exactly between two odd numbers.) (Series) 4 1 7 9 2 2 7 9 9 9 4 7 7 8 9 1 8 6 2 1 2 2 8 (DIGIT). How many such even numbers are there, each of which has an odd number placed exactly before it and an odd number placed exactly after it?

On the basis of the given arrangement of letters, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and form a group. Which one is NOT related to that group in the same way as the others?
